title: Task File
description: JSON task file generated by task-producing skills (to-tasks, debug, improve-component, to-e2e-tasks, etc.). Version 2: 'prd' field removed; required 'producer' and 'source' fields added via cross-file $ref to provenance-schema.

schema_version.description: Always '2'. Hard cutover from v1 — old files fail validation by design.

producer.description: The skill that generated this task file.

source.description: What the task file was generated from. {type:'seed', ref:<path>} when generated from a seed. {type:'prd', ref:<path>} when generated from an HTML PRD. {type:'session', ref:null} when generated directly from a grill/debug/review session.

strategy.description: 'single': all tasks run on one branch named in branching.branch. 'per-task': each task uses its own task.branch value.

branch.description: Branch name. Required when strategy is 'single'. Omit when strategy is 'per-task'.

branching.description: Branching strategy for run-tasks execution. See branching-strategy.md for derivation rules.

id.description: Globally unique, zero-padded to 4 digits (T-0001, T-0002, ...). Assigned by next-task-id.sh.

type.description: AFK: can be implemented and merged without human interaction. HITL: requires a keyboard action the AI cannot perform (e.g. enable a feature flag, add a credential, configure DNS). Decision tasks and design reviews are NOT HITL — they are resolved in the grill and never become tasks.

description.description: End-to-end behavior description. Not layer-by-layer. For refactor tasks: must state that characterization tests for existing behavior are written BEFORE any restructuring begins.

acceptance_criteria.description: Verifiable criteria. At least one must be test-related.

blocked_by.description: IDs of tasks that must be done or merged before this task can start.

status.description: Current execution state managed by run-tasks.

branch.description: Branch name for per-task strategy; null for single-branch strategy.

pr.description: PR URL or number once merged; null until then.

url_path.description: Route to check. Must start with /. Dynamic segments as :param placeholders (e.g. /firms/:firmSlug/dashboard).

assertions.description: Observable behaviors — visible text, navigation targets, redirects. Not implementation details.

browser_verify.description: Optional browser verification after TDD passes. Omit for non-UI tasks (backend, config, refactor, infra). E2e-authoring tasks from to-e2e-tasks must never carry browser_verify.

linear_url.description: Linear issue URL written back by tasks-to-linear after issue creation.

task_type.description: Runner selector. 'code': routed to build-code (inline-skill). 'triage': routed to export-tasks (spawn-agent). Optional — omit for legacy files.

domain.description: Destination zone for routing. Matches a key in resources/task-routing.json destinations map. Optional — omit for legacy files.

deliverable.description: Classifies the task output. 'code': output is a PR or code change. 'non-code': output is a note, research item, or non-code artifact. Optional — omit for legacy files.

export_url.description: Written back by export-tasks after a successful external write (GitHub issue URL, Notion page URL, etc.). null until then. Optional — omit for legacy files.

seed_ref.description: Seed filename (basename only, no directory prefix) from which this triage task was generated. Written by to-tasks for triage items. Optional — omit for code tasks and legacy files.

task_ref.description: Task file filename (basename only, no directory prefix) in which this triage task lives. Written by to-tasks for triage items. Optional — omit for code tasks and legacy files.

tasks.description: Array of task objects to execute in order.

source_branch.description: Source branch this e2e task file was generated from. Written by to-e2e-tasks only. Optional.

gate_override.description: Written by to-tasks when the user confirms a sanctioned gate override. Records what was bypassed and why. The seed's verification.status is never changed by this field.

reason.description: Human-readable description of what was bypassed.

confirmed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the user confirmed the override.

id.description: Sequential within this file, zero-padded to 3 digits (FU-001, FU-002, ...).

title.description: Short description of the manual action.

steps.description: Ordered steps. Each must be specific: exact command, exact SQL, exact config key, or exact dashboard click path. Never write 'update X' without specifying where and how.

trigger_task.description: Task ID (e.g. T-0024) whose completion creates the need for this follow-up, or null if general.

source.description: 'planned': identified during seed/PRD/debug session. 'discovered': found during run-tasks execution.

follow_ups.description: Manual follow-up actions required after task execution (env var provisioning, DNS config, DB migrations, etc.).

tasks.title: Bootstrap JWT signing infrastructure
tasks.description: Add RS256 key pair generation, JWT signing, and expiry-validation utilities. This is the foundation all auth tasks depend on.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover signToken(), verifyToken(), and expiry edge cases
signToken() throws when private key is absent
verifyToken() returns null for expired tokens, not an error

tasks.title: Design review: token revocation strategy (HITL)
tasks.description: The team must decide the revocation strategy for compromised refresh tokens before the refresh rotation task can be implemented. Options: denylist in Redis, short-lived tokens only, or instant-revoke via DB flag.
acceptance_criteria: Revocation strategy decision is recorded in docs/adr/
T-0025 acceptance criteria updated to reflect chosen approach

tasks.title: Refresh token rotation endpoint
tasks.description: POST /auth/refresh accepts a valid refresh token (HttpOnly cookie), issues a new access + refresh pair, and invalidates the old refresh token per the chosen revocation strategy.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test hits a real Postgres refresh token table (no mocks)
Old refresh token is invalidated on successful rotation
Browser check: visiting /dashboard with an expired access token redirects to /login

follow_ups.title: Provision RS256 key pair in secrets manager
steps: Generate key pair: openssl genrsa -out private.pem 2048 && openssl rsa -in private.pem -pubout -out public.pem
Store private key: aws secretsmanager create-secret --name jwt-private-key --secret-string file://private.pem
Store public key: aws secretsmanager create-secret --name jwt-public-key --secret-string file://public.pem
Delete local key files: rm private.pem public.pem
Add JWT_PRIVATE_KEY_SECRET_ARN and JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_SECRET_ARN to the app's environment config
